source: Where To Next - A Dynamic Model of User Preferences

concepts.finding: Dynamic preference model for sequential recommendation (location check-ins). User preference = weighted combination of (1) long-term static preferences, (2) short-term recent transitions, (3) periodic patterns (weekly routines). Tensor factorization models user-time-item interactions. Outperforms static MF and Markov-chain models by 15-25%.
concepts.implication: Dynamic keyword intent model: keyword intent = weighted combination of (1) static long-term intent (always about cars), (2) short-term session intent (currently about repair), (3) periodic intent patterns (financial keywords on weekdays, entertainment on weekends).
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal weighting between static, session, and periodic components for keyword intent prediction?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A dynamic keyword intent model (static + session + periodic components) outperforms static-only models by 10%+ on time-dependent keyword intent classification.
